title: Handling Product Returns
description: How to process returns correctly while staying legal and conflict-free with customers.

steps.title: Meeting a customer with a return
steps.content: Return customers often arrive already frustrated. Don't argue or make excuses. Greet them, smile, say: 'Let's take a look at what happened'. Guide them to a separate area (not the checkout, to avoid blocking the queue). The first 30 seconds set the tone for the whole interaction.
steps.key_takeaway: Separate return customers from the queue. A conflict at the register is seen by 10 others.

steps.title: Checking legal grounds
steps.content: Per most consumer protection laws: non-food goods in proper condition — return window varies (typically 14-30 days depending on jurisdiction and store policy). The item didn't fit in: shape, size, style, color, dimensions, configuration. Food items — no return unless defective. Receipt is preferred but alternative proof (bank statement) often accepted.
steps.key_takeaway: Know your return window and store policy by heart. 'Didn't fit' is usually valid.

steps.title: Inspecting merchandise condition
steps.content: For a return, the item must be: unused, with preserved merchantable condition (seals, tags, packaging), with consumer properties intact. Usage signs, scratches, odor — refusal with explanation. Inspect in front of the customer, describe aloud what you see.
steps.key_takeaway: Inspecting aloud with the customer = transparency. Silent refusal = complaint.

steps.title: Items NOT eligible for return
steps.content: Typical non-returnable categories: prescription drugs, hygiene items, underwear and hosiery, perfume (sealed only), jewelry, vehicles, firearms, plants, animals, books and print. Memorize the list — customers often 'try their luck'.
steps.key_takeaway: Jewelry, underwear, perfume, medications — no returns. It's law, not store whim.

steps.title: Completing the return form
steps.content: Customer fills out a return form: full name, ID (number), phone, item name, purchase date, return reason, refund method (cash / card / mobile). No ID = no cash refund. Form signed by customer + cashier. Copy to customer.
steps.key_takeaway: No ID, no cash refund. It's tax law, not store preference.

steps.title: Refund processing
steps.content: Cash — immediate, if the register holds enough. Card — refund to the same card (1-10 business days by law, usually 1-3). Mobile — same day. Never give cash if payment was by card — that's a tax violation with store fines. Print the return receipt.
steps.key_takeaway: Card → card. Cash → cash. Cross-method refunds = up to $500 fine.

steps.title: Logging and exchange/upsell
steps.content: Log into the system: item, reason, outcome. After the return, offer: exchange for another item, discount on next purchase, promo subscription. A customer whose return was handled smoothly returns more often than one who never had a return.
steps.key_takeaway: Smooth return = +1 loyal customer. Conflict return = -5 through reviews.

quiz.question: Return window for non-food items in proper condition?
quiz.explanation: Varies by country and store policy — know yours by heart.

quiz.question: Which item is NOT returnable?
quiz.explanation: Jewelry is on the typical exclusion list, along with underwear, perfume, medications.

quiz.question: Can a return be processed without a receipt?
options: Yes, but harder — needs alternative proof
quiz.explanation: Most laws allow return without receipt if other proof exists (bank statement, witness).

quiz.question: Payment was by card — how to refund?
quiz.explanation: Same card only — tax requirement. Cross-method refund triggers a store fine.

quiz.question: What to do with a frustrated customer returning an item?
options: Lead them to a separate area, don't argue
quiz.explanation: Conflict at the register is seen by other customers. Separation protects reputation.

manager_checklist: Staff leads customer away from the register to a separate area
Knows the return window by heart
Inspects merchandise aloud in front of the customer
Refunds via the same method as payment
Offers exchange or upsell after return

kpi.how_to_measure: Timestamp from contact to return receipt
